coaching.title: Hill Repeats — 12 mi
coaching.message: Peak Phase, Week 13 of 22. Today is the big vert stimulus: 2 mi easy warm-up, then 14x 90-sec hard uphills with a walk-down recovery, then 2 mi cool-down. Alternate reps — run some all-out (drive Zone 4, near 165+ bpm LTHR on the hard efforts), power-hike others at max effort with hands on quads and short punchy strides. This is exactly the Julian Alps demand: ~2500m of alpine climbing where power hiking is a weapon, not a fallback. Buenos Aires is flat, so find the steepest sustained ramp, parking garage, or stadium steps you can and repeat it. Full walk-down recovery every rep — quality of the climb matters more than rushing the reset.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: This is a quality session — arrive fueled. Take 40-60g carbs 60-90 min pre-run (toast + banana + honey, or a rice-based meal). It's under 90 min of work so a single gel or ~30g carbs mid-session is plenty, but keep sipping to stay ahead of the Buenos Aires effort. Within 30 min of finishing, hit the recovery window hard: ~30g protein plus 60-80g carbs to refill glycogen after all those hard climbs. Keep training the gut on race-day products now while stakes are low.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Hill repeats hammer the calves, glutes, and Achilles eccentrically on every walk-down. Tonight: 10 min easy spin or walk to flush, then foam roll calves and quads and gently mobilize ankles. Elevate the legs for a bit and prioritize 8+ hours of sleep — the adaptation from today's vert stimulus is locked in overnight. If anything in the Achilles or calf feels sharp tomorrow, keep the next session easy and flat.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Peak Phase is where the race gets won on the climbs. Every one of those 14 reps is a rehearsal for a Julian Alps switchback at hour four when your legs are already deep. Pick a cue — 'strong and patient' — and run it on each hard interval. You're 64 days out and building the exact engine this course rewards. Embrace the grind on the flats of Buenos Aires; the mountains will feel like coming home.
